title: Unit 05 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Investing, Risk, and Wealth Building.

questions.prompt: What is investing mainly intended to do?

choices.text: Help money grow over time
choices.feedback: Correct. Investing is meant to support financial growth over time.

choices.text: Remove all financial risk permanently
choices.feedback: Investing does not remove all risk.

choices.text: Guarantee immediate profit in every case
choices.feedback: Investing does not guarantee immediate or certain profit.

choices.text: Replace the need for long-term planning
choices.feedback: Investing is stronger when it is part of long-term planning.

questions.prompt: Time can make investment growth stronger.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Time can increase the opportunity for investment growth.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Longer time periods can strengthen growth outcomes.

questions.prompt: Which investment type is most closely connected to company ownership?

choices.text: Stocks
choices.feedback: Stocks are the strongest match, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Bonds
choices.feedback: Bonds are associated with lending rather than company ownership.

choices.text: Stocks
choices.feedback: Correct. Stocks are associated with ownership in a company.

choices.text: Savings fee
choices.feedback: A savings fee is not an investment type.

questions.prompt: What does diversification mean in investing?

choices.text: Putting all money into one investment only
choices.feedback: That is the opposite of diversification.

choices.text: Spreading investments across more than one option
choices.feedback: This is the strongest definition, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Avoiding all long-term planning
choices.feedback: Diversification is part of investment planning, not the avoidance of it.

choices.text: Spreading investments across more than one option
choices.feedback: Correct. Diversification means not depending on one investment alone.

questions.prompt: Risk and return should both be considered in investment decisions.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong investment reasoning considers possible growth and possible risk together.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Risk and return are both important in investment analysis.

questions.prompt: Why can diversification be a stronger strategy than relying on one investment only?

choices.text: Because it can reduce the risk of depending on one result
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reason, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Because it guarantees that no investment will ever lose value
choices.feedback: Diversification can reduce some risk, but it does not guarantee no loss.

choices.text: Because it removes the need to compare investment choices
choices.feedback: Investment choices still need to be compared carefully.

choices.text: Because it can reduce the risk of depending on one result
choices.feedback: Correct. Diversification helps spread risk rather than concentrating it in one choice.

questions.prompt: If $700 grows to $790 over time, how much growth occurred?

choices.feedback: Subtract the starting value from the later value carefully.
This is the correct amount, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.
Correct. $790 minus $700 equals $90 growth.
The growth amount is slightly lower than $100.

questions.prompt: Which is the strongest reason to start investing earlier rather than later?

choices.text: Because earlier investing can give money more time to grow
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reason, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Because earlier investing can give money more time to grow
choices.feedback: Correct. Time can strengthen long-term growth opportunities.

choices.text: Because later investing never allows any growth
choices.feedback: Later investing can still allow growth, though the time period may be shorter.

choices.text: Because earlier investing removes all investment risk
choices.feedback: Earlier investing does not remove all risk.
